[[File:Dragon axe detail.png|left|140px]]
The '''dragon axe''' is the second strongest and fastest [[axe]] in the game after its direct upgrade, the [[crystal axe]], and is tied with the [[3rd age axe]]. It is 10% more efficient at chopping logs than the [[rune axe]].{{CiteNews|url=https://services.runescape.com/m=news/song-of-the-elves-poll-blog-i?oldschool=1|title=Song of the Elves Poll Blog I|newsdate=28 January 2019}}

Like all axes, it is both a [[weapon]] (requiring 60 [[Attack]] to wield) and a [[tool]] (requiring 61 [[Woodcutting]] to use).

As with most [[dragon equipment]], the dragon axe '''cannot''' be made from raw materials using the [[Smithing]] skill.

==Success rates==
Every 4 ticks, one chance of obtaining a log is rolled. The success rate for chopping a log is approximately 385% that of the [[bronze axe]], or 10% better than a [[rune axe]].

{{Skilling success chart|label=Dragon axe success chances|showbefore=yes

==Special attack ==
[[File:Lumber Up.gif|frame|A player performs the dragon axe's special attack, ''Lumber Up''.]]
The dragon axe has a [[Special attacks|special attack]], ''Lumber Up'', that it shares with the [[Infernal axe|infernal]], [[3rd age axe|3rd age]], and [[Crystal axe|crystal axes]] and their variants. It [[Temporary skill boost|temporarily boosts]] the player's [[Woodcutting]] level by 3, consuming 100% of the player's special attack energy. The boost degrades every 60 seconds, with the player's Woodcutting level returning to normal after 3 minutes. Using [[Preserve]] can increase the duration by 50%.

The special attack can be used to allow players to chop with the axe itself starting at level 58 Woodcutting, although it cannot be used without the level 60 [[Attack]] required to wield the axe.

Note that any levels boosted above 99 with the special attack will be treated as 99. Therefore, you will start to receive diminishing returns with the special attack at level 97 and 98{{CiteTwitter|author=Mod Ash|url=https://twitter.com/JagexAsh/status/1375844352098975753|date=27 March 2021|archiveurl=https://reldo.runescape.wiki/i/98237|archivedate=29 March 2021}}, with visible boosts past level 99 only influencing [[clue nest]] chances.{{CiteTwitter|author=Mod Ash|url=https://twitter.com/JagexAsh/status/1573018832998109185|date=22 September 2022|archiveurl=https://reldo.runescape.wiki/i/102590|archivedate=10 June 2023|quote=The formula for dropping clue nests only benefits from visible boosts. So the +7 invisible one won't help, but a visible boost would help beyond 99 if you can get one.}} Like the [[dragon harpoon]], the dragon axe's special attack does '''not''' disrupt resource gathering if the player is already chopping when the special attack is activated.

==Upgrading ==
[[File:Smouldering stone.gif|right|frame|A player uses a smouldering stone to create an infernal axe.]]
Players can combine a [[smouldering stone]] with a dragon axe to create an [[infernal axe]], which requires level 85 [[Firemaking]] (cannot be boosted) to do so. This process '''cannot''' be undone and the resultant axe '''cannot''' be traded. The infernal axe will grant the player a 1/3 chance to burn logs for Firemaking experience whilst cutting. The infernal axe will be able to burn 5,000 logs before degrading, requiring another smouldering stone or dragon axe to charge it.

Players can also create a [[crystal axe]] via a [[singing bowl]] by combining a dragon axe with 120 [[crystal shard]]s and a [[crystal tool seed]]. This requires level 76 [[Smithing]] and [[Crafting]], which can be [[Temporary skill boost|boosted]]. If players cannot create it on their own, they can have [[Conwenna]] or [[Reese]] sing the crystal for them for an extra 60 crystal shards, for a total of 180.
